title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
snippets: Skin T cells consist of the three subtypes: cytotoxic (Tc) expressing CD8A/B, helper (Th) expressing CD4, CD40LG and regulatory (Treg) expressing FOXP3, TIGIT, CTLA4

In atopic dermatitis (AD), cytotoxic T cells expressing IL13, IL22 and IFNG (Tc IL13/IL22) and in psoriasis, T cells expressing IL17A, IL17F, IFNG, IL22 and IL26 (Tc17/Th17) are found in both lesional and non-lesional skin, but are significantly enriched in lesional skin

Tc IL13/IL22 cells are dominant in the dermis of AD lesional skin and express amphiregulin (AREG), a member of the epidermal growth factor family, skin tissue residency genes (RGS1, NR4A1, NR4A2), and effector and activated T cell genes (ID2, PRDM1, MAP3K8, DUSP2)

Tc17/Th17 cells are dominant in the epidermis of lesional psoriasis skin, which was validated by flow cytometry. These cells express genes characteristic of activated and pathogenic Th17 cells (KLRB1, RBPJ and CXCL13)
Lesional clonal T cells had higher expression of co-stimulation genes (CD63, TNFRSF18, JAML) and T cell receptor signaling (EVL, LAT, LCK, JAK1) than non-clonal T cells

Epidermal T cells upregulated genes associated with skin tissue residency (RGS1, PPP1R15A), effector memory (CD44, ID2), T cell activation (TNFRSF18) and inhibition of T cell response (CD96, TSC22D3 and DUSP4)
ILC=innate lymphoid cell, NK=natural killer cell, Tc=cytotoxic T cell, Th=T helper cell, Treg=regulatory T cell

title: A Transcriptomic Atlas of Healthy Human Skin Links Regional Identity to Inflammatory Disease
snippets: CD8 T cells exhibited cytotoxic signatures (CD8A, CD8B, GZMA, GNLY)

title: Granzyme A potentiates chemokine production in IL-17-stimulated keratinocytes
snippets: the majority of CD8 T cells from psoriasis lesions expressed GzmA. Confocal microscopy confirmed intracellular GzmA+ granules in epidermal T cells
